sections.label: Section 3.440
sections.original:
ἀλκᾷ · νῦν δʼ ἀπολείπομαι
τᾶς εὐδαίμονος ἥβας .
sections.translation: to champion them ; but now my happy youth is gone and I am left .

sections.label: Section 442
sections.original:
— ἀλλʼ ἐσορῶ γὰρ τούσδε φθιμένων
ἔνδυτʼ ἔχοντας , τοὺς τοῦ μεγάλου
δή ποτε παῖδας τὸ πρὶν Ἡρακλέους ,
sections.translation: But look ! I see the children of Heracles who was once so great , wearing the clothes of the dead ,

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original:
ἄλοχόν τε φίλην ὑπὸ σειραίοις
ποσὶν ἕλκουσαν τέκνα , καὶ γεραιὸν
πατέρʼ Ἡρακλέους . δύστηνος ἐγώ ,
δακρύων ὡς οὐ δύναμαι κατέχειν
sections.translation: and his loving wife dragging her babes along at her side , and Heracles ’ aged father . Ah ! woe is me ! no longer can I stem the flood of tears

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: γραίας ὄσσων ἔτι πηγάς .
sections.translation: that spring to my old eyes .

sections.label: Section 451
sections.original:
εἶἑν · τίς ἱερεύς , τίς σφαγεὺς τῶν δυσπότμων
ἢ τῆς ταλαίνης τῆς ἐμῆς ψυχῆς φονεύς ;
ἕτοιμʼ ἄγειν τὰ θύματʼ εἰς Ἅιδου τάδε .
ὦ τέκνʼ , ἀγόμεθα ζεῦγος οὐ καλὸν νεκρῶν ,
sections.translation: Come now , who is to sacrifice or butcher these poor children ? or rob me of my wretched life ? These victims are ready to be led to Hades ’ halls . O my children ! an ill-matched company are we hurried off to die ,

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original:
ὁμοῦ γέροντες καὶ νέοι καὶ μητέρες .
ὦ μοῖρα δυστάλαινʼ ἐμή τε καὶ τέκνων
τῶνδʼ , οὓς πανύστατʼ ὄμμασιν προσδέρκομαι ⋯
ἔτεκον μὲν ὑμᾶς , πολεμίοις δʼ ἐθρεψάμην
ὕβρισμα κἀπίχαρμα καὶ διαφθοράν .
sections.translation: old men and young ones and mothers , all together . Alas ! for my sad fate and my children ’ s , whom these eyes now for the last time behold . So I gave you birth and reared you only for our foes to mock , to jeer at , and slay .

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original:
φεῦ ·
ἦ πολύ με δόξης ἐξέπαισαν ἐλπίδες ,
ἣν πατρὸς ὑμῶν ἐκ λόγων ποτʼ ἤλπισα .
σοὶ μὲν γὰρ Ἄργος ἔνεμʼ ὁ κατθανὼν πατήρ ,
Εὐρυσθέως δʼ ἔμελλες οἰκήσειν δόμους
τῆς καλλικάρπου κράτος ἔχων Πελασγίας ,
στολήν τε θηρὸς ἀμφέβαλλε σῷ κάρᾳ
sections.translation: Ah me ! how bitterly my hopes have disappointed me in the expectation I once formed from the words of your father . Addressing each of her three sons in turn . To you your dead father was for giving Argos ; and you were to dwell in the halls of Eurystheus , lording it over the fair fruitful land of Argolis ;

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original:
λέοντος , ᾗπερ αὐτὸς ἐξωπλίζετο ·
σὺ δʼ ἦσθα Θηβῶν τῶν φιλαρμάτων ἄναξ ,
ἔγκληρα πεδία τἀμὰ γῆς κεκτημένος ,
ὡς ἐξέπειθες τὸν κατασπείραντά σε ·
ἐς δεξιάν τε σὴν ἀλεξητήριον
sections.translation: and over your head would he throw that lion ’ s skin with which he himself was armed . And you were to be king of Thebes , famed for its chariots , receiving as your heritage my broad lands , for so you coaxed your dear father ;

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original:
ξύλον καθίει δαίδαλον , ψευδῆ δόσιν .
σοὶ δʼ ἣν ἔπερσε τοῖς ἑκηβόλοις ποτὲ
τόξοισι δώσειν Οἰχαλίαν ὑπέσχετο .
τρεῖς δʼ ὄντας ὑμᾶς τριπτύχοις τυραννίσι
sections.translation: and to your hand he used to resign the carved club , his sure defence , pretending to give it to you . And to you he promised to give Oechalia , which once his archery had wasted . Thus with three principalities

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original:
πατὴρ ἐπύργου , μέγα φρονῶν εὐανδρίᾳ ·
ἐγὼ δὲ νύμφας ἠκροθινιαζόμην ,
κήδη συνάψουσʼ , ἔκ τʼ Ἀθηναίων χθονὸς
Σπάρτης τε Θηβῶν θʼ , ὡς ἀνημμένοι κάλῳς
πρυμνησίοισι βίον ἔχοιτʼ εὐδαίμονα .
sections.translation: would your father exalt you , his three sons , proud of your manliness ; while I was choosing the best brides for you , scheming to link you by marriage to Athens , Thebes , and Sparta , that you might live a happy life with a fast sheet-anchor to hold by .

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original:
καὶ ταῦτα φροῦδα · μεταβαλοῦσα δʼ ἡ τύχη
νύμφας μὲν ὑμῖν Κῆρας ἀντέδωκʼ ἔχειν ,
ἐμοὶ δὲ δάκρυα λουτρά — δύστηνος φρενῶν .
πατὴρ δὲ πατρὸς ἑστιᾷ γάμους ὅδε ,
Ἅιδην νομίζων πενθερόν , κῆδος πατρός .
ὤμοι , τίνʼ ὑμῶν πρῶτον ἢ τίνʼ ὕστατον
sections.translation: And now that is all vanished ; fortune ’ s breeze has veered and given to you for brides the maidens of death in their stead , and my tears will be the marriage bath ; woe is me for my foolish thoughts ! and your grandfather here is celebrating your marriage-feast , the cares of a father , accepting Hades as the father of your brides .

sections.label: Section 485
sections.translation: Ah me ! which of you shall I first press to my bosom , which last ? on which bestow my kiss , or clasp close to me ? Oh ! would that like the bee with russet wing , I could collect from every source my sighs in one , and , blending them together , shed them in one copious flood !

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original:
θνητῶν παρʼ Ἅιδῃ , σοὶ τάδʼ , Ἡράκλεις , λέγω ·
θνῄσκει πατὴρ σὸς καὶ τέκνʼ , ὄλλυμαι δʼ ἐγώ ,
ἣ πρὶν μακαρία διὰ σʼ ἐκλῃζόμην βροτοῖς .
ἄρηξον , ἐλθέ · καὶ σκιὰ φάνηθί μοι ·
ἅλις γὰρ ἐλθὼν κἂν ὄναρ γένοιο σύ ·
sections.translation: O my dearest Heracles , to you I call , if perhaps mortal voice can make itself heard in Hades ’ halls ; your father and children are dying , and I am doomed , I who once because of you was counted blessed as men count bliss . Come to our rescue ; appear , I pray , if only as a phantom ,

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original:
κακοὶ γάρ εἰσιν οἳ τέκνα κτείνουσι σά .
σὺ μὲν τὰ νέρθεν εὐτρεπῆ ποιοῦ , γύναι ·
sections.translation: since your arrival , even as a dream , would be enough , for they are cowards who are slaying your children .

sections.label: Section 497
sections.original:
ἐγὼ δὲ σέ , ὦ Ζεῦ , χεῖρʼ ἐς οὐρανὸν δικὼν
αὐδῶ , τέκνοισιν εἴ τι τοισίδʼ ὠφελεῖν
sections.translation: Lady , prepare the funeral rites ; but I , O Zeus , stretching out my hand to heaven , call on you to help these children ,

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original:
μέλλεις , ἀμύνειν , ὡς τάχʼ οὐδὲν ἀρκέσεις .
καίτοι κέκλησαι πολλάκις · μάτην πονῶ ·
θανεῖν γάρ , ὡς ἔοικʼ , ἀναγκαίως ἔχει .
ἀλλʼ , ὦ γέροντες , μικρὰ μὲν τὰ τοῦ βίου ,
τοῦτον δʼ ὅπως ἥδιστα διαπεράσετε ,
sections.translation: if such is your intention ; for soon any aid of yours will be unavailing ; and yet you have been often invoked ; my toil is in vain ; death seems inevitable . You aged friends , the joys of life are few ; so take heed that you pass through it as gladly as you may ,

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original:
ἐξ ἡμέρας ἐς νύκτα μὴ λυπούμενοι .
ὡς ἐλπίδας μὲν ὁ χρόνος οὐκ ἐπίσταται
σῴζειν , τὸ δʼ αὑτοῦ σπουδάσας διέπτατο .
ὁρᾶτʼ ἔμʼ ὅσπερ ἦ περίβλεπτος βροτοῖς
ὀνομαστὰ πράσσων , καί μʼ ἀφείλεθʼ ἡ τύχη
sections.translation: without a thought of sorrow from morning until night ; for time takes little heed of preserving our hopes ; and , when he has busied himself on his own business , away he flies . Look at me , a man who had made a mark among his fellows by deeds of note ; yet fortune in a single day

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original:
ὥσπερ πτερὸν πρὸς αἰθέρʼ ἡμέρᾳ μιᾷ .
ὁ δʼ ὄλβος ὁ μέγας ἥ τε δόξʼ οὐκ οἶδʼ ὅτῳ
βέβαιός ἐστι . χαίρετʼ · ἄνδρα γὰρ φίλον
πανύστατον νῦν , ἥλικες , δεδόρκατε .
sections.translation: has robbed me of it as of a feather that floats away toward the sky . I know not any whose plenteous wealth and high reputation is fixed and sure ; fare you well , for now you have seen the last of your old friend , my comrades .

sections.label: Section 514
sections.original:
ἔα ·
ὦ πρέσβυ , λεύσσω τἀμὰ φίλτατʼ · ἢ τί φῶ ;
sections.translation: Ah ! Old friend , is it my own , my dearest I see ? or what am I to say ?

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα , θύγατερ · ἀφασία δὲ κἄμʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: I do not know , my daughter ; I too am struck dumb .

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original:
ὅδʼ ἐστὶν ὃν γῆς νέρθεν εἰσηκούομεν ,
εἰ μή γʼ ὄνειρον ἐν φάει τι λεύσσομεν .
τί φημί ; ποῖʼ ὄνειρα κηραίνουσʼ ὁρῶ ;
οὐκ ἔσθʼ ὅδʼ ἄλλος ἀντὶ σοῦ παιδός , γέρον .
sections.translation: Is this he who they told us was beneath the earth ? It is , unless some day-dream mocks our sight . What am I saying ? What visions do these anxious eyes behold ? Old man , this is no one other than your own son .

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original:
δεῦρʼ , ὦ τέκνʼ , ἐκκρίμνασθε πατρῴων πέπλων ,
ἴτʼ ἐγκονεῖτε , μὴ μεθῆτʼ , ἐπεὶ Διὸς
σωτῆρος ὑμῖν οὐδέν ἐσθʼ ὅδʼ ὕστερος .
sections.translation: Come here , my children , cling to your father ’ s robe , hurry , never loose your hold , for here is one to help you , not at all behind our savior Zeus .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original:
ὦ χαῖρε , μέλαθρον πρόπυλά θʼ ἑστίας ἐμῆς ,
ὡς ἄσμενός σʼ ἐσεῖδον ἐς φάος μολών .
sections.translation: All hail ! my house and gates of my home , how glad I am to emerge to the light and see you .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original:
ἔα · τί χρῆμα ; τέκνʼ ὁρῶ πρὸ δωμάτων
στολμοῖσι νεκρῶν κρᾶτας ἐξεστεμμένα ,
ὄχλῳ τʼ ἐν ἀνδρῶν τὴν ἐμὴν ξυνάορον ,
πατέρα τε δακρύοντα — συμφορὰς τίνας ;
φέρʼ ἐκπύθωμαι τῶνδε πλησίον σταθείς ·
sections.translation: Ah ! what is this ? I see my children before the house in the robes of death , with chaplets on their heads and my wife amid a throng of men , and my father weeping — what misfortune ? Let me draw near to them and inquire ;

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: γύναι , τί καινὸν ἦλθε δώμασιν χρέος ;
sections.translation: lady , what strange stroke of fate has fallen on the house ?

sections.label: Section 531
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἀνδρῶν ⋯
sections.translation: Dearest of all mankind to me !

sections.label: Section 531b
sections.original: ὦ φάος μολὼν πατρί ⋯
sections.translation: O ray of light appearing to your father !

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: ἥκεις , ἐσώθης εἰς ἀκμὴν ἐλθὼν φίλοις ;
sections.translation: Are you safe and is your coming just in time to help your dear ones ?

sections.label: Section 533
sections.original: τί φῄς ; τίνʼ ἐς ταραγμὸν ἥκομεν , πάτερ ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? What is this confusion I find on my arrival , father ?

sections.label: Section 534
sections.original: διολλύμεσθα · σὺ δέ , γέρον , σύγγνωθί μοι ,
sections.translation: We are being ruined ; forgive me , old friend ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original:
εἰ πρόσθεν ἥρπασʼ ἃ σὲ λέγειν πρὸς τόνδʼ ἐχρῆν ·
τὸ θῆλυ γάρ πως μᾶλλον οἰκτρὸν ἀρσένων ,
καὶ τἄμʼ ἔθνῃσκε τέκνʼ , ἀπωλλύμην δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: if I have anticipated that to which you had a right to tell him ; for women ’ s nature is perhaps more prone to grief than men ’ s and they are my children that were being led to death , which was my own lot too .

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: Ἄπολλον , οἵοις φροιμίοις ἄρχῃ λόγου .
sections.translation: Apollo ! what a prelude to your story !

sections.label: Section 539
sections.original: τεθνᾶσʼ ἀδελφοὶ καὶ πατὴρ οὑμὸς γέρων .
sections.translation: My brothers are dead , and my old father .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; τί δράσας ἢ δορὸς ποίου τυχών ;
sections.translation: How so ? what did he do ? whose spear did he meet ?

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: Λύκος σφʼ ὁ καινὸς γῆς ἄναξ διώλεσεν .
sections.translation: Lycus , our new monarch , slew him .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: ὅπλοις ἀπαντῶν ἢ νοσησάσης χθονός ;
sections.translation: Did he meet him in fair fight , or was the land sick and weak ?

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: στάσει · τὸ Κάδμου δʼ ἑπτάπυλον ἔχει κράτος .
sections.translation: Yes , from faction ; now he is master of the city of Cadmus with its seven gates .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: τί δῆτα πρὸς σὲ καὶ γέροντʼ ἦλθεν φόβος ;
sections.translation: Why has panic fallen on you and my aged father ?

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: κτείνειν ἔμελλε πατέρα κἀμὲ καὶ τέκνα .
sections.translation: He meant to kill your father , me , and my children .

sections.label: Section 546
sections.original: τί φῄς ; τί ταρβῶν ὀρφάνευμʼ ἐμῶν τέκνων ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? What did he have to fear from my orphan babes ?

sections.label: Section 547
sections.original: μή ποτε Κρέοντος θάνατον ἐκτεισαίατο .
sections.translation: He was afraid they might some day avenge Creon ’ s death .

sections.label: Section 548
sections.original: κόσμος δὲ παίδων τίς ὅδε νερτέροις πρέπων ;
sections.translation: What is this dress they wear , suited to the dead ?

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: θανάτου τάδʼ ἤδη περιβόλαιʼ ἀνήμμεθα .
sections.translation: It is the garb of death we have already put on .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: καὶ πρὸς βίαν ἐθνῄσκετʼ ; ὦ τλήμων ἐγώ .
sections.translation: And were you being forced to die ? O woe is me !

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: φίλων γʼ ἔρημοι · σὲ δὲ θανόντʼ ἠκούομεν .
sections.translation: Yes , deserted by every friend , and informed that you were dead .

sections.label: Section 552
sections.original: πόθεν δʼ ἐς ὑμᾶς ἥδʼ ἐσῆλθʼ ἀθυμία ;
sections.translation: What put such desperate thoughts into your heads ?

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: Εὐρυσθέως κήρυκες ἤγγελλον τάδε .
sections.translation: That was what the heralds of Eurystheus kept proclaiming .

sections.label: Section 554
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐξελείπετʼ οἶκον ἑστίαν τʼ ἐμήν ;
sections.translation: Why did you leave my hearth and home ?

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: βίᾳ , πατὴρ μὲν ἐκπεσὼν στρωτοῦ λέχους ⋯
sections.translation: He forced us ; your father was dragged from his bed .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: κοὐκ ἔσχεν αἰδῶ τὸν γέροντʼ ἀτιμάσαι ;
sections.translation: Had he no shame , to ill-use the old man so ?

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: αἰδώς γʼ ἀποικεῖ τῆσδε τῆς θεοῦ πρόσω .
sections.translation: Shame indeed ! that goddess and he dwell far enough apart .

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: οὕτω δʼ ἀπόντες ἐσπανίζομεν φίλων ;
sections.translation: Was I so poor in friends in my absence ?

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: φίλοι γάρ εἰσιν ἀνδρὶ δυστυχεῖ τίνες ;
sections.translation: Who are the friends of a man in misfortune ?

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: μάχας δὲ Μινυῶν ἃς ἔτλην ἀπέπτυσαν ;
sections.translation: Do they make so light of my hard warring with the Minyans ?

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: ἄφιλον , ἵνʼ αὖθίς σοι λέγω , τὸ δυστυχές .
sections.translation: Misfortune , to repeat it to you , has no friends .

sections.label: Section 562
sections.translation: Cast from your heads these chaplets of death , look up to the light , for instead of the darkness below your eyes behold the welcome sun .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original:
ἐγὼ δέ — νῦν γὰρ τῆς ἐμῆς ἔργον χερός —
πρῶτον μὲν εἶμι καὶ κατασκάψω δόμους
καινῶν τυράννων , κρᾶτα δʼ ἀνόσιον τεμὼν
ῥίψω κυνῶν ἕλκημα · Καδμείων δʼ ὅσους
κακοὺς ἐφηῦρον εὖ παθόντας ἐξ ἐμοῦ ,
sections.translation: I , meanwhile , since here is work for my hand , will first go raze this upstart tyrant ’ s halls , and when I have beheaded the villain , I will throw him to dogs to tear ; and every Theban who I find has played the traitor after my kindness ,

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original:
τῷ καλλινίκῳ τῷδʼ ὅπλῳ χειρώσομαι ·
τοὺς δὲ πτερωτοῖς διαφορῶν τοξεύμασι
νεκρῶν ἅπαντʼ Ἰσμηνὸν ἐμπλήσω φόνου ,
Δίρκης τε νᾶμα λευκὸν αἱμαχθήσεται .
τῷ γάρ μʼ ἀμύνειν μᾶλλον ἢ δάμαρτι χρὴ
sections.translation: will I destroy with this victorious club ; the rest will I tear apart with my feathered shafts and fill Ismenus full of bloody corpses , and Dirce ’ s clear stream shall run red with gore . For whom ought I to help rather than wife

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original:
καὶ παισὶ καὶ ⋯ γέροντι ; χαιρόντων πόνοι ·
μάτην γὰρ αὐτοὺς τῶνδε μᾶλλον ἤνυσα .
καὶ δεῖ μʼ ὑπὲρ τῶνδʼ , εἴπερ οἵδʼ ὑπὲρ πατρός ,
θνῄσκειν ἀμύνοντʼ · ἢ τί φήσομεν καλὸν
ὕδρᾳ μὲν ἐλθεῖν ἐς μάχην λέοντί τε
sections.translation: and children and aged father ? Farewell my labors ! for it was in vain I accomplished them rather than helping these . And yet I ought to die in their defence , since they for their father were doomed ; or what shall we find so noble in having fought a hydra and a lion

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original:
Εὐρυσθέως πομπαῖσι , τῶν δʼ ἐμῶν τέκνων
οὐκ ἐκπονήσω θάνατον ; οὐκ ἄρʼ Ἡρακλῆς
ὁ καλλίνικος ὡς πάροιθε λέξομαι .
sections.translation: at the commands of Eurystheus , if I make no effort to save my own children from death ? No longer then , as before , shall I be called Heracles the victor .
